---
title: "NYC Citywide Payroll Data Analysis"
author: "Shawn Ganz"
date: today
format: 
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    code-fold: true
    theme: cosmo
jupyter: python3
---

## Initialization

```{python}
#| echo: false
#| include: false

import os
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import seaborn as sns
import requests
from dotenv import load_dotenv
from pathlib import Path
from great_tables import GT


load_dotenv()

NYC_API = "https://data.cityofnewyork.us/api/v3/views/k397-673e/query.json"

data_science_titles = [
    "Data Analyst",
    "City Research Scientist",
    "Research Scientist",
    "Statistician",
    "IT Project Specialist"
]

core_exact_titles = [
    "CITY RESEARCH SCIENTIST",
    "IT PROJECT SPECIALIST",
    "STATISTICIAN"
]

similar_titles = [
    "CITY RESEARCH SCIENTIST",
    "IT PROJECT SPECIALIST",
    "STATISTICIAN",
    "BUSINESS RESEARCH & DATA ANALYTICS SPECIALIST",
    "BUSINESS DATA AND REPORTING ANALYST",
    "CRIME ANALYST",
    "CYBER SECURITY ANALYST",
    "IT SECURITY SPECIALIST",
    "SENIOR IT ARCHITECT",
    "IT INFRASTRUCTURE ENGINEER",
    "IT AUTOMATION AND MONITORING ENGINEER",
    "COMPUTER PROGRAMMER ANALYST",
    "COMPUTER SPECIALIST",
    "COMPUTER ASSOCIATE",
    "ADMINISTRATIVE STAFF ANALYST",
    "ASSOCIATE STAFF ANALYST",
    "STAFF ANALYST",
    "CERTIFIED IT DEVELOPER",
    "DATA ANALYST",
    "SENIOR DATA ANALYST"
]

```

```{python}
def check_endpoint(base_api_url, app_token):
    headers = {
        'X-App-Token': app_token,
        'Content-Type': 'application/json'
    }
    
    # Test with small limit in URL
    url = f"{base_api_url}?$limit=1"
    
    response = requests.post(url, headers=headers)
    
    if response.status_code == 200:
        print("Endpoint is working")
        return True
    else:
        print(f"Endpoint failed. Status: {response.status_code}")
        print(f"Message: {response.text}")
        return False


def fetch_data(base_api_url, app_token):
    all_data = []
    offset = 0
    limit = 200000
    headers = {
        'X-App-Token': app_token,
        'Content-Type': 'application/json'
    }
    
    while True:
        url = f"{base_api_url}?$limit={limit}&$offset={offset}"
        
        response = requests.post(url, headers=headers)
        response.raise_for_status()
        
        batch = pd.DataFrame(response.json())
        all_data.append(batch)
        
        if len(batch) < limit:
            break
            
        offset += limit
    
    return pd.concat(all_data, ignore_index=True)


def load_or_fetch_data(base_api_url=None, csv_filename="nyc_payroll_data.csv"):
    csv_path = Path(csv_filename)
    
    if csv_path.exists():
        return pd.read_csv(csv_path, low_memory=False)
    
    app_token = os.getenv("app_token")
    if not app_token:
        raise ValueError("app_token is missing from .env")
    
    if base_api_url is None:
        raise ValueError("base_api_url is required when fetching from API")
    
    df = fetch_data(base_api_url, app_token)
    df.to_csv(csv_path, index=False)
    return df

def clean_money_column(series):
    """Clean a money column (remove $, commas, convert to float)"""
    return (series
            .astype(str)
            .str.replace('$', '', regex=False)
            .str.replace(',', '', regex=False)
            .str.strip()
            .replace('', pd.NA)
            .astype(float))

```

```{python}
df_raw = load_or_fetch_data(
    base_api_url=NYC_API, 
    csv_filename="nyc_payroll_data.csv"
)

df_raw.info()
```

```{python}
# Start simple
df_new = (
    df_raw
    .reset_index(drop=True)
    .assign(row_id=lambda x: x.index)
)

# Select columns and rename first (cleaner column names)
df = df_new[[
    'Agency Name', 
    'Title Description', 
    'First Name', 
    'Last Name',
    'Pay Basis',
    'Base Salary', 
    'Regular Gross Paid', 
    'Total OT Paid', 
    'Total Other Pay',
    'Fiscal Year', 
    'Leave Status as of June 30',
    'Agency Start Date',
    'Work Location Borough'
]].copy()

# Rename to snake_case
df = df.rename(columns={
    'Agency Name': 'agency_name',
    'Title Description': 'title_description',
    'First Name': 'first_name',
    'Last Name': 'last_name',
    'Pay Basis': 'pay_basis',
    'Base Salary': 'base_salary',
    'Regular Gross Paid': 'regular_gross_paid',
    'Total OT Paid': 'total_ot_paid',
    'Total Other Pay': 'total_other_pay',
    'Fiscal Year': 'fiscal_year',
    'Leave Status as of June 30': 'leave_status',
    'Agency Start Date': 'agency_start_date',
    'Work Location Borough': 'work_location_borough'
})

# Clean money columns
money_cols = ['base_salary', 'regular_gross_paid', 'total_ot_paid', 'total_other_pay']

for col in money_cols:
    if col in df.columns:
        df[col] = clean_money_column(df[col])

# Now apply filters using the new column names
df = (
    df
    .loc[df['leave_status'] == "ACTIVE"]
    .loc[df['fiscal_year'] == 2025]
    .loc[df['pay_basis'] == 'per Annum']        # only per Annum
    .loc[df['base_salary'] >= 20000]      # remove junk/low placeholders
    .loc[df['base_salary'] != 1]
)

df.info()
```

```{python}
salary_sorted = df["base_salary"].sort_values().reset_index(drop=True)

plt.figure(figsize=(10, 5))

plt.scatter(salary_sorted.index, salary_sorted, alpha=0.3)

plt.xlabel("Sorted Row Index")
plt.ylabel("Base Salary")
plt.title("Sorted Base Salary Scatterplot")

plt.show()
```

```{python}
plt.figure(figsize=(12, 7))

# Seaborn histogram with density (normalized)
sns.histplot(
    data=df, 
    x='base_salary', 
    bins=80, 
    kde=False,           # adds smooth density curve
    stat='density',     # normalized
    edgecolor='black',
    alpha=0.8
)

plt.title('Distribution of Base Salary — Normalized with KDE', fontsize=14)
plt.xlabel('Base Salary ($)', fontsize=12)
plt.ylabel('Density', fontsize=12)
plt.grid(True, alpha=0.3)

# Stats box
stats = df['base_salary'].describe()
plt.text(0.95, 0.95, f"Count: {len(df):,}\nMean: ${stats['mean']:,.0f}\nMedian: ${stats['50%']:,.0f}", 
         transform=plt.gca().transAxes, ha='right', va='top',
         bbox=dict(boxstyle="round", facecolor="white", alpha=0.9))

plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```

Which agencies spend the most on employee compensation?

```{python}
# Group by agency and calculate summary statistics
agency_total_base_salary = (
    df.groupby("agency_name")
      .agg(
          total_base_salary=("base_salary", "sum"),
          mean_base_salary=("base_salary", "mean"),
          median_base_salary=("base_salary", "median"),
          employee_count=("base_salary", "count")
      )
      .sort_values("total_base_salary", ascending=False)
)

# Get top 20 agencies by total base salary
top_agencies_salary_table = agency_total_base_salary.head(10).reset_index()

# === Nicely formatted pandas Styler table ===
styled_table = (
    top_agencies_salary_table
    .style
    .format({
        "total_base_salary": "${:,.0f}",
        "mean_base_salary": "${:,.0f}",
        "median_base_salary": "${:,.0f}",
        "employee_count": "{:,.0f}"
    })
    .set_caption("Top 10 NYC Agencies by Total Base Salary<br>"
                 "<small>Fiscal Year 2025 • Active annual employees</small>")
    .set_table_styles([
        {'selector': 'caption', 
         'props': [('text-align', 'center'), 
                   ('font-size', '16px'), 
                   ('font-weight', 'bold')]},
        {'selector': 'th', 'props': [('text-align', 'left')]},
        {'selector': 'td', 'props': [('text-align', 'right')]}
    ])
    .hide(axis="index")   # Hide default row numbers
)

styled_table
```

```{python}
plot_data = top_agencies_salary_table.sort_values("total_base_salary")

plt.figure(figsize=(10, 6))

plt.barh(
    plot_data["agency_name"],
    plot_data["total_base_salary"]
)

plt.title("Top 10 NYC Agencies by Total Base Salary")
plt.xlabel("Total Base Salary ($)")
plt.ylabel("Agency")

plt.gca().xaxis.set_major_formatter(
    plt.FuncFormatter(lambda x, _: f"${x/1e9:.1f}B")
)

plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```
Which agencies have the highest average salaries? -

```{python}

# Group by agency and calculate the required metrics
agency_salary_summary = (
    df.groupby("agency_name")
      .agg(
          mean_base_salary=("base_salary", "mean"),
          median_base_salary=("base_salary", "median"),
          total_base_salary=("base_salary", "sum"),
          employee_count=("base_salary", "count")
      )
)

# Filter to agencies with at least 50 employees
agency_salary_summary_50plus = agency_salary_summary[
    agency_salary_summary["employee_count"] >= 50
].copy()

# Get top 20 by average base salary
top_agencies_avg_salary = (
    agency_salary_summary_50plus
    .sort_values("mean_base_salary", ascending=False)
    .head(10)
    .reset_index()
)

# === Pretty display using pandas styling ===
styled_table = (
    top_agencies_avg_salary
    .style
    .format({
        "mean_base_salary": "${:,.0f}",
        "median_base_salary": "${:,.0f}",
        "total_base_salary": "${:,.0f}",
        "employee_count": "{:,.0f}"
    })
    .set_caption("Top 10 NYC Agencies by Average Base Salary<br>"
                 "<small>Active annual employees, fiscal year 2025 • agencies with ≥ 50 employees</small>")
    .set_table_styles([
        {'selector': 'caption', 'props': [('text-align', 'center'), 
                                          ('font-size', '16px'), 
                                          ('font-weight', 'bold')]},
        {'selector': 'th', 'props': [('text-align', 'left')]},
        {'selector': 'td', 'props': [('text-align', 'right')]}
    ])
    .hide(axis="index")  # hide the default numeric index
)

styled_table
```
```{python}
plot_data = top_agencies_avg_salary.sort_values("mean_base_salary", ascending=False)

plt.figure(figsize=(10, 6))

ax = sns.barplot(
    data=plot_data,
    x="mean_base_salary",
    y="agency_name"
)

plt.title("Top 10 NYC Agencies by Average Base Salary")
plt.xlabel("Average Base Salary ($)")
plt.ylabel("Agency")

# Truncate axis to show differences more clearly
plt.xlim(100000, 145000)

ax.xaxis.set_major_formatter(
    plt.FuncFormatter(lambda x, _: f"${x/1000:.0f}K")
)

# Add labels to bars
for container in ax.containers:
    ax.bar_label(
        container,
        labels=[f"${v/1000:.0f}K" for v in plot_data["mean_base_salary"]],
        padding=4
    )

plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```
Among selected data science-related civil service titles, which agencies offer the highest compensation?

```{python}
df_core = df[df['title_description'].isin(core_exact_titles)].copy()
df_similar = df[df['title_description'].isin(similar_titles)].copy()
```

```{python}
# Agency-level salary summaries for core and similar title groups
core_agency_salary = (
    df_core
    .groupby("agency_name")
    .agg(
        mean_base_salary=("base_salary", "mean"),
        median_base_salary=("base_salary", "median"),
        employee_count=("base_salary", "count"),
        total_base_salary=("base_salary", "sum")
    )
    .loc[lambda x: x["employee_count"] >= 10]
    .sort_values("mean_base_salary", ascending=False)
)

similar_agency_salary = (
    df_similar
    .groupby("agency_name")
    .agg(
        mean_base_salary=("base_salary", "mean"),
        median_base_salary=("base_salary", "median"),
        employee_count=("base_salary", "count"),
        total_base_salary=("base_salary", "sum")
    )
    .loc[lambda x: x["employee_count"] >= 10]
    .sort_values("mean_base_salary", ascending=False)
)
```

```{python}
top_core_agency_salary = core_agency_salary.head(10).reset_index()

top_core_agency_salary.style.format({
    "mean_base_salary": "${:,.0f}",
    "median_base_salary": "${:,.0f}",
    "employee_count": "{:,.0f}",
    "total_base_salary": "${:,.0f}"
}).set_caption(
    "Top Agencies by Average Base Salary: Core Data Science-Related Titles"
)
```
```{python}
plot_data = top_core_agency_salary.sort_values("mean_base_salary", ascending=False)

plt.figure(figsize=(10, 6))

ax = sns.barplot(
    data=plot_data,
    x="mean_base_salary",
    y="agency_name"
)

plt.title("Top Agencies by Average Base Salary: Core Data Science-Related Titles")
plt.xlabel("Average Base Salary ($)")
plt.ylabel("")

plt.xlim(100000, 160000)

ax.xaxis.set_major_formatter(
    plt.FuncFormatter(lambda x, _: f"${x/1000:.0f}K")
)

for container in ax.containers:
    ax.bar_label(
        container,
        labels=[f"${v/1000:.0f}K" for v in plot_data["mean_base_salary"]],
        padding=4
    )

plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```

```{python}
top_similar_agency_salary = similar_agency_salary.head(10).reset_index()

top_similar_agency_salary.style.format({
    "mean_base_salary": "${:,.0f}",
    "median_base_salary": "${:,.0f}",
    "employee_count": "{:,.0f}",
    "total_base_salary": "${:,.0f}"
}).set_caption(
    "Top Agencies by Average Base Salary: Similar Data/Analytics/IT-Related Titles"
)
```

```{python}
plot_data = top_similar_agency_salary.sort_values("mean_base_salary", ascending=False)

plt.figure(figsize=(10, 6))

ax = sns.barplot(
    data=plot_data,
    x="mean_base_salary",
    y="agency_name"
)

plt.title("Top Agencies by Average Base Salary: Similar Data/Analytics/IT-Related Titles")
plt.xlabel("Average Base Salary ($)")
plt.ylabel("")

plt.xlim(100000, 150000)

ax.xaxis.set_major_formatter(
    plt.FuncFormatter(lambda x, _: f"${x/1000:.0f}K")
)

for container in ax.containers:
    ax.bar_label(
        container,
        labels=[f"${v/1000:.0f}K" for v in plot_data["mean_base_salary"]],
        padding=4
    )

plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```

```{python}
top_core_jobs = (
    df_core
    .groupby("title_description")
    .agg(
        mean_base_salary=("base_salary", "mean"),
        median_base_salary=("base_salary", "median"),
        employee_count=("base_salary", "count")
    )
    .sort_values("mean_base_salary", ascending=False)
    .head(5)
    .reset_index()
)

top_core_jobs.style.format({
    "mean_base_salary": "${:,.0f}",
    "median_base_salary": "${:,.0f}",
    "employee_count": "{:,.0f}"
}).set_caption(
    "Top Core Data Science-Related Titles by Mean Base Salary"
)
```
```{python}
plot_data = top_core_jobs.sort_values("mean_base_salary", ascending=False)

plt.figure(figsize=(10, 4))

ax = sns.barplot(
    data=plot_data,
    x="mean_base_salary",
    y="title_description"
)

plt.title("Core Data Science-Related Titles by Mean Base Salary")
plt.xlabel("Mean Base Salary ($)")
plt.ylabel("")

plt.xlim(0, 140000)

ax.xaxis.set_major_formatter(
    plt.FuncFormatter(lambda x, _: f"${x/1000:.0f}K")
)

for container in ax.containers:
    ax.bar_label(
        container,
        labels=[f"${v/1000:.0f}K" for v in plot_data["mean_base_salary"]],
        padding=4
    )

plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```


```{python}
top_similar_jobs = (
    df_similar
    .groupby("title_description")
    .agg(
        mean_base_salary=("base_salary", "mean"),
        median_base_salary=("base_salary", "median"),
        employee_count=("base_salary", "count")
    )
    .sort_values("mean_base_salary", ascending=False)
    .head(10)
    .reset_index()
)

top_similar_jobs.style.format({
    "mean_base_salary": "${:,.0f}",
    "median_base_salary": "${:,.0f}",
    "employee_count": "{:,.0f}"
}).set_caption(
    "Top 10 Similar Data/Analytics/IT-Related Titles by Mean Base Salary"
)
```

```{python}
plot_data = top_similar_jobs.sort_values("mean_base_salary", ascending=False)

plt.figure(figsize=(10, 5))

ax = sns.barplot(
    data=plot_data,
    x="mean_base_salary",
    y="title_description"
)

plt.title("Top 10 Similar Data/Analytics/IT-Related Titles by Mean Base Salary")
plt.xlabel("Mean Base Salary ($)")
plt.ylabel("")

plt.xlim(50000, 200000)

ax.xaxis.set_major_formatter(
    plt.FuncFormatter(lambda x, _: f"${x/1000:.0f}K")
)

for container in ax.containers:
    ax.bar_label(
        container,
        labels=[f"${v/1000:.0f}K" for v in plot_data["mean_base_salary"]],
        padding=4
    )

plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```
